tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), with significant proteinuria and ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, suggesting no hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Findings are consistent with possible chronic kidney disease and protein-losing nephropathy; consider further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
